raw.population: Initial N: 201 (Spain) Age: 9.5 (1 – 14) WT: 35 (9-78) Validation N: 74 (Spain) Age: 9.8 (1-14) WT: 39.8 (11-70)
raw.objective: “evaluate the effects of sex, total body weight (TBW), age, polytherapy, and CBZ dose on CBZ clearance (CL) in children by using sparse data collected during routine clinical care.”
raw.covariates: WT, age, CBZ dose, phenobarbital comedication on CL . WT on Vd
raw.observations: Large residual variance, model seems accurate (unbiased) but imprecise. Although the model seems to predict well for the population, much unexplained variability remains. Dosing guidelines provided by the authors should probably be followed up with TDM and Bayesian dose adjustments
raw.equations: CL/F=(theta1*WT + theta2*Dose)*(Age**theta3)*(1+theta4*PHB)*(1+eta1) Vd/F=theta5*WT Ka=theta6 F=0.85 Cobs=Cipred + eps1 Dose=CBZ dose (mg/kg/day) PHB= comedication with phenobarbital alone (yes =1, no=0)
raw.parameter_values: theta1=0.0122 theta2=0.0467 theta3=0.331 theta4=0.289 theta5=1.5 theta6=0.65 omega1=0.118 sigma1=1.52

extraction_target.title: Carbamazepine population pharmacokinetics in children: mixed-effect models.
extraction_target.population: Initial N: 201 (Spain) Age: 9.5 (1 – 14) WT: 35 (9-78) Validation N: 74 (Spain) Age: 9.8 (1-14) WT: 39.8 (11-70)

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

sigma.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale SIGMA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.residual_error: CL/F=(theta1*WT + theta2*Dose)*(Age**theta3)*(1+theta4*PHB)*(1+eta1) Vd/F=theta5*WT Ka=theta6 F=0.85 Cobs=Cipred + eps1 Dose=CBZ dose (mg/kg/day) PHB= comedication with phenobarbital alone (yes =1, no=0)
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.population: Initial N: 201 (Spain) Age: 9.5 (1 – 14) WT: 35 (9-78) Validation N: 74 (Spain) Age: 9.8 (1-14) WT: 39.8 (11-70)
study.objective: “evaluate the effects of sex, total body weight (TBW), age, polytherapy, and CBZ dose on CBZ clearance (CL) in children by using sparse data collected during routine clinical care.”

tested: phenobarbital comedication on CL . WT on Vd
covariates.raw: WT, age, CBZ dose, phenobarbital comedication on CL . WT on Vd

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
